summary: Power is how fast work is done or energy is delivered, P = A / t. The same work done in less time means more power.
method: Find the work done or energy transferred, A.
Find the time interval t over which it happens.
Decide which quantity is unknown — power, work, or time.
Rearrange P = A / t for the unknown.
Substitute in SI units (J, s) so the power comes out in watts.

example.title: Power of a winch
steps: The unknown is P, so use P = A / t.
